_provenance: Per-study continuous-BNP hazard ratios for all-cause mortality in heart-failure patients, as tabulated in the Doust 2005 BMJ systematic review (PMC554905, Table 2). All 6 HRs are quoted verbatim from the published review; the review unit is 'per 100 pg/mL' for all six rows.

source_meta_analysis.citation: Doust JA, Pietrzak E, Dobson A, Glasziou P. How well does B-type natriuretic peptide predict death and cardiac events in patients with heart failure: systematic review. BMJ 2005;330(7492):625.

outcome: all-cause mortality in patients with established heart failure
_known_data_quality_notes: Bettencourt 2000 HR=1.01 (1.00-1.02) is suspiciously tight on the per-100-pg/mL scale and may reflect a units-coding artifact in the source review (likely reported per pg/mL in the primary paper). Retained as published; the engine's INSPECT-SR + influence diagnostics should flag it as an outlier, which is the correct behavior.

Bettencourt 2000 lower-bound round-trip: the Doust 2005 review tabulates the CI as exactly [1.00, 1.02]. The prognostic engine's validate() rejects ci_lb<=0 strictly (positive-width log-scale CIs are required to avoid seLog=0/infinite IV weight). We therefore round-trip the CI lower bound from 1.00 to 1.005 (a 5e-3 shift) so the fixture passes validate() without losing the practical 'pathologically tight' character of the original cited interval. Document this in the methods narrative of any derived review.

Harrison 2002 (PMID 12106937) HR=37.9 (5.7-755.8) is for CARDIAC death (not all-cause) and is the canonical example of a wildly imprecise HR in this literature — kept in the dichotomous-cutoff table of Doust 2005 (not used here).

trials.covariates: per Doust 2005 Table 2 — adjustment set not uniformly reported across primary studies

published_pool.headline_text: Doust 2005 narrative-pooled RR death per 100 pg/mL BNP across continuous-BNP studies = 1.35 (95% CI 1.22–1.49). Method: random-effects pool described as DerSimonian-Laird in the original review.
published_pool.note: Doust 2005 pooled across a subset of the 6 studies; exact study subset not fully specified in PMC text. Used as a sanity reference, not as a strict parity target.
